video_title: How to Make a Ginger Bug for Natural Soda | Wild Fermentation from Scratch
text:
Method for ginger bug starter 🍹✨

Ingredients:
350 ml drink water 
1 tbsp unpeeled fresh ginger, chopped (choose organic!)
1 tbsp cane sugar

Method:
1️⃣ Chop the unpeeled ginger into small pieces (don’t peel, because the skin has wild yeasts!).

2️⃣ Add the water, ginger, and sugar to a clean glass jar. Stir vigorously to dissolve the sugar and introduce oxygen.

3️⃣ Cover loosely with a cloth or lid and keep at room temperature.

4️⃣ Every 24 hours, feed with 1 tbsp chopped ginger and 1 tbsp sugar. Stir well.

5️⃣ Repeat daily until you see bubbles, a slightly yeasty smell, and fizz when stirred — usually after 3–5 days.

✨ Once active, store in the fridge and feed once a week.

video_title: Making PINE NEEDLE SODA
text:
Back in the American revolution, conscripts were typically rationed beer or even malt (to make beer) as part of their total food supply. This even accounted towards horses, as horses were given a type of malted drink to eat as a food supplement. 

However, back then hops weren't really a thing then. Or maybe to the higher-class folk it was, but not to soldiers. Soldiers would make something called Gruet when times got rough, and it usually involved harvesting the juniper leaves and pine leaves from the surrounding forest, add malt, water and molasses/sugar. 

They would keep that concoction in a sort of rotational way where soldiers would consistently have a batch of gruet in the making. In that movie "The Patriot", in the dark night scene in the beginning of the movie, the soldiers in the background are concocting a batch of gruet. 

I used to have the manifest for the ration distribution from the late 1700s, but have since lost it due to some data transferring issues, although information about it is online. 

I LOVE learning about what soldiers did to cook for themselves. Even towards the end of the American Revolution, the colonists would raid English supply lines, and take the rations. It showed they were in such disarray in the winter times...

Many soldiers lost their lives to malnutrition alone in many of those wars back then. It was recipes like this that actually kept soldiers going in times of dismay.

text: I have a B.S. in Biochemistry from a state school, so clearly I’m speaking with some gravitas 😂  The answer to neutralizing acetaldehyde is aldehyde dehydrogenase, which is an enzyme that cannot be effectively delivered via supplements. It would disintegrate into amino acids before reaching your liver. But if you eat a diet high in various B vitamins, zinc, magnesium - they directly support the function of aldehyde dehydrogenase. That’s the secret. You have to eat things that you probably don’t want to eat and your body will work its magic and make the hangover less severe. video_title: Making PINE NEEDLE SODA
text:
Back in the American revolution, conscripts were typically rationed beer or even malt (to make beer) as part of their total food supply. This even accounted towards horses, as horses were given a type of malted drink to eat as a food supplement. 

However, back then hops weren't really a thing then. Or maybe to the higher-class folk it was, but not to soldiers. Soldiers would make something called Gruet when times got rough, and it usually involved harvesting the juniper leaves and pine leaves from the surrounding forest, add malt, water and molasses/sugar. 

They would keep that concoction in a sort of rotational way where soldiers would consistently have a batch of gruet in the making. In that movie "The Patriot", in the dark night scene in the beginning of the movie, the soldiers in the background are concocting a batch of gruet. 

I used to have the manifest for the ration distribution from the late 1700s, but have since lost it due to some data transferring issues, although information about it is online. 

I LOVE learning about what soldiers did to cook for themselves. Even towards the end of the American Revolution, the colonists would raid English supply lines, and take the rations. It showed they were in such disarray in the winter times...

Many soldiers lost their lives to malnutrition alone in many of those wars back then. It was recipes like this that actually kept soldiers going in times of dismay.

video_title: Ich teste Produkte, über die sich das Internet streitet 🤬😤 Super Pop, Oatly Matcha, vegan Taste Test
text: Ich liebe die Super Pop Getränke - ich liebe aber auch Bitter Lemon und allgemein Getränke die "außergewöhnliche" Geschmäcke haben, dafür kann man mich mit ganz süß jagen - z.B. Fanta o.ä.

video_title: Ich teste Produkte, über die sich das Internet streitet 🤬😤 Super Pop, Oatly Matcha, vegan Taste Test
text: Hi Inken, ich hab mir tatsächlich heute in der Mittagspause ein SuperPop gekauft, Geschmacksrichtung Passionfruit Mango. Und ich fands beim ersten Schluck genauso schlimm wie du😂 aber nach ein paar Schlücken gehts, mich stört eher der Süßungsmittelgeschmack.

video_title: Ich teste Produkte, über die sich das Internet streitet 🤬😤 Super Pop, Oatly Matcha, vegan Taste Test
text: Ich habe die Frikadellen aus Versehen mit heißem Wasser aufgegossen und sie hatten einfach null Bindung 🙈 wir haben dann Hackfleisch gehabt statt Frikadellen 😅 also erklären kann ich es dir nicht, aber kaltes Wasser funktioniert offenbar besser 😄 also wer gerne Hack möchte: nehmt warmes Wasser, war auch lecker 😄

video_title: Ich teste Produkte, über die sich das Internet streitet 🤬😤 Super Pop, Oatly Matcha, vegan Taste Test
text: Deine Haare sehen in letzter Zeit irgendwie besonders toll aus😍😍

video_title: Ich teste Produkte, über die sich das Internet streitet 🤬😤 Super Pop, Oatly Matcha, vegan Taste Test
text: Ich habe mir bei Super Pop auch etwas ganz anderes drunter vorgestellt. Einfach, dass die Geschmacksrichtung intensiver ist. Ich mag kein Apfelessig und hatte echt große Hoffnungen, gerade weil es so gehyped wird. Naja… 🙈😅

video_title: Ich teste Produkte, über die sich das Internet streitet 🤬😤 Super Pop, Oatly Matcha, vegan Taste Test
text: Ich mag deine Testvideos gerne🥰ich hab Super Pop im Kühlschrank, ebenso den Oatly Matcha aber beides noch nicht probiert. Bin gespannt bei deinem Gesicht wg dem Apfelessig, hätte jetzt auch eher gedacht das wäre sehr dezent wahrnehmbar.

video_title: Ich teste Produkte, über die sich das Internet streitet 🤬😤 Super Pop, Oatly Matcha, vegan Taste Test
text:
Ich mag solche Drinks eh nicht, also hab ich mir auch schon gedacht, dass ich das nicht mag 😂 die Frikadellen hab ich Lustigerweise auch schon länger rumstehen, weil ich irgendwie die Zubereitung bei denen verwirrend zu lesen fand 😂
Den Matcha liebe ich!!!

video_title: my everyday matcha latte
text:
the perfect iced matcha latte imo. everything I used below!! 

- 12-15g honey
- 1/8 tsp kosher salt
- 1/8 tsp ground vanilla
- 3.2g – 4g matcha (I use Ippodo sayaka for everyday and ummon for special occasions)
- 80 g warm water at 115°F
- 100 g creamy milk (I use my house blend milk)

full recipe + stuff i couldn’t say in a 30 second video: https://munchingwithmariyah.com/iced-matcha-latte/

video_title: my everyday matcha latte
text: Ngl I just throw water and matcha in a thermos and shake for a minute. The microfoam is crazy.

video_title: my everyday matcha latte
text: Key to getting microfoam: 1) Use 1 TBSP of room temp water to whisk the matcha into a paste. THEN add 3-4 TBSP of 176F water into the bowl. 2) Vigorously flick the wrist as you whisk for 15 seconds and then another 15 seconds whisking just the top of the liquid until you see the desired microfoam.

video_title: my everyday matcha latte
text: Try whisking the matcha before the honey to get the microfoam! Then add the honey once it’s whisked up 😊

video_title: my everyday matcha latte
text: My first time on this channel, where the creator is measuring all ingredients and so are the commentators 😂 woww, the pursuit of perfection!

video_title: my everyday matcha latte
text: maple syrup easily blends well too also its like low calorie if you care about that too

video_title: my everyday matcha latte
text: I love adding a little salt to my matcha or coffee. I make a saline solution I keep in a dropper vial so I can add a precise amount to cold drinks as well as hot. just dissolve 16g flaky salt into 50g scalding water, it lasts me a couple weeks of daily drinks.

video_title: my everyday matcha latte
text: The microfoam is in the way you hold the whisk. Straighten your pointer finger and hold it firmly vertically on the whisk with your pointer finger faces down towards the tines and then you can hold your bowl or the whisk at an angle and whisk quickly

text:
While George Crum was a real person and did serve thin fried potatoes, he didn’t necessarily invent them. Recipes for thin, fried potato slices predate 1853 — there’s one in a British cookbook from 1822 and another in an American cookbook from 1832.

So, Crum popularized the potato chip — especially in Saratoga Springs — but probably wasn’t the first to make them.

text:
How potato chips were invented

In 1853, in New York, USA, a chef named George Crum worked in a restaurant. One day, a customer complained that Crum’s fried potatoes were too thick and kept sending them back.

So Crum got annoyed and decided to slice the potatoes super thin, fry them until they were very crispy, and add extra salt—just to teach the customer a lesson.

But surprisingly…

👉 The customer loved it!
👉 Other people loved it too.
👉 Soon, the crispy thin potatoes became popular and were called potato chips.

Fun facts

Potato chips were first called “Saratoga Chips.”

They became popular all over America, then the whole world.

Today potato chips are one of the most popular snacks ever!

video_title: Strawberry Refrigerator Cake #recipe #strawberryrefrigeratorcake #strawberrycake #strawberryboxcake
text:
Strawberry Refrigerator Cake 

1 box strawberry cake mix
3 eggs and oil for box cake
1  box of instant vanilla pudding 
1 cup of milk 
Cool whip thawed 
Fresh Strawberries - sliced 
Frozen strawberries - thawed and puréed 

Bake cake according to packaging.  Allow cake to cool. Poke the cake with holes with the handle of a wooden spoon.

Pour puréed strawberries over the cake. Top strawberry slices.

Add pudding and milk to a large bowl. Whip until combined, until the consistency of a pudding. Fold in Cool Whip. Spread topping over cake. 

Be sure to refrigerate. Serve cake cold.

video_title: Trying 3 American School Lunches
text: The best school lunch I ever had was in elementary school in Puerto Rico (2002-2008). No microwaved food, it was all cooked from scratch by the cafeteria ladies. Breakfast: Freshly made oatmeal, cream of wheat, fresh fruits, etc.. Lunch: Anything from Rice, beans, and chicken to chinese rice. Fried chicken, beef, vegetables, etc.. On "special days" we would get hot dogs, or rarely pizza. Definitely miss those times, even more when I moved out of Puerto Rico in 2011.

video_title: Making PINE NEEDLE SODA
text:
Back in the American revolution, conscripts were typically rationed beer or even malt (to make beer) as part of their total food supply. This even accounted towards horses, as horses were given a type of malted drink to eat as a food supplement. 

However, back then hops weren't really a thing then. Or maybe to the higher-class folk it was, but not to soldiers. Soldiers would make something called Gruet when times got rough, and it usually involved harvesting the juniper leaves and pine leaves from the surrounding forest, add malt, water and molasses/sugar. 

They would keep that concoction in a sort of rotational way where soldiers would consistently have a batch of gruet in the making. In that movie "The Patriot", in the dark night scene in the beginning of the movie, the soldiers in the background are concocting a batch of gruet. 

I used to have the manifest for the ration distribution from the late 1700s, but have since lost it due to some data transferring issues, although information about it is online. 

I LOVE learning about what soldiers did to cook for themselves. Even towards the end of the American Revolution, the colonists would raid English supply lines, and take the rations. It showed they were in such disarray in the winter times...

Many soldiers lost their lives to malnutrition alone in many of those wars back then. It was recipes like this that actually kept soldiers going in times of dismay.

video_title: Why Farmers Feed Molasses to Cows
text: The stomachs of cows and other ruminants work on fermentation. Sometimes grass and other feeds cannot be fermented quickly enough if there is a lack of sugar or if the feed is dry; reducing the nutrient absorption of the cows. Give them some molasses (sugars) and the bacteria can start multiplying easily, which in turn eases the breakdown of those heavy cellulose compounds!

video_title: YOUR SILK PRESS SUCKS! #naturalhair #silkpress
text:
My never fails Silk press routine
-Wash with a good clarifying/detoxing shampoo
-Go in with a MOISTURIZING hair masque or deep conditioner
-Wash AGAIN after deep conditioning 

Blow drying
-Apply heat protectant (try to find one that’s very light in the hair) 
-Apply anti humidity/anti frizz products 
(My favorites are:Paul Mitchell skinny serum, bio silk, kenra 5 and 19, and Amika the wizard)
- Blow dry in small sections making sure to get the hair as straight as possible before moving on. 
WAIT at least 2 hours before straightening

When straightening apply a little more serum or anti humidity product to the hair. Use high heat (don’t be afraid) and small sections. When done wrapping the hair does wonders 
The key to a good silk press is clean moisturized hair !!

video_title: Silk Review for Marvel Champions The Card Game
text:
We had a rough to bad time with her and it reminded me a bit of psylocke. There is a fun hero in there but not for everyone. 

When I see others post about her I don’t fully grasp how easy everyone acts about getting her engine up and running, my experience was closer to yours and we had a few bad games where it took forever to make her effective. What drove us crazy was matching the specific encounter sets which was incredibly fiddly. We hated her against thunderbolts (at four player) for that reason. I’m glad she’s in the game but not really a card for us. 

Great point about player side schemes as well, j Jonah makes that strategy way more fun. At large multiplayer the effort it takes to clear a player side scheme is a lot, so having him slowly chip down on one makes that a lot more fun.

video_title: Silk Review for Marvel Champions The Card Game
text: I am 100% with you on Silk. I bought her out of curiosity because of the unique design, expecting her to feel too clunky or convoluted to actually be fun. But from the very first game I’ve loved the way she plays. Lots of interesting decisions to make which can lead up to some big turns. Really like how thematic the tuck mechanic feels as well- learning from what the villain does to get stronger. It might be recency bias but she’s a top 3 heroes for me at the mo.

video_title: Silk Review for Marvel Champions The Card Game
text: I’ve just picked her up as the tucking mechanic looked interesting to me. Yet to play but looking forward to it.

video_title: Silk Review for Marvel Champions The Card Game
text:
I've only played her 5 times so far, and all in multiplayer. My son always likes to shuffle in one to two extra encounter sets, so she may have been worse because of that, lol. She felt a little tough getting setup sometimes. She did amazing in a couple of games, but I felt like I was the weak link most of the time at the table. My wife with Shuri seemed to really be carrying!

It was also a protection deck, which I think adding that extra setup on a setup heavy character was likely a mistake. 

Need to play her again for sure.

video_title: Silk Review for Marvel Champions The Card Game
text:
She is clearly one on my favorites! The game design is so clever! And she can do absolutely anything!
Personally I tuck (almost) every card since you can ready and/or draw 2 cards. And I play her upgrades in the second deck pass. Albert Moon is the goat! On turn one you can have your money back! You play him, tuck one and draw two!!!

video_title: Silk Review for Marvel Champions The Card Game
text: Took a few games for Silk to click, but once she did she quickly snuck into my top 10. I found Smooth as Silk to be occasionally useful. It can help a bit with early ramp up, or get villain cards tucked in scenarios that include a few different encounter sets. Not a card I'd use every game, but it can help smooth her kit out when things aren't aligning as you'd like.

video_title: Silk Review for Marvel Champions The Card Game
text: I love silk,  but i also understamd the pain of getting a major headache after playing her

video_title: Silk Review for Marvel Champions The Card Game
text:
I use Smooth as Silk almost every time it gets into my hand. I typically just choose the villain to get a card from the villains encounter set which guarantees me my 9 damage swinging silk kicks since I use the majority of the tucked cards to her alter-ego card draw ability. Get the Scoop I pretty much only use as a resource and her upgrades I don't use all the time minus the thwart one that readies her and the attack one.

I love Silk with Suit Up and Moongirl plus her alter-ego card draw ability and web-warrior allies just leads to absurdly explosive turns. Silk's probably my favourite hero of this wave so far when I thought it would be Nick Fury or Shuri. I'm hoping I like Winter Soldier as much as I do Silk lol.

video_title: Silk Review for Marvel Champions The Card Game
text: Silk is one of my favourite marvel characters so happy she's in the game. After a few games, it gets much easier to play her imo. Some tips are: Don't feel the need to get all her upgrades, most of the time, outwit is not needed.😢 spider claws is really only maybe needed in the end game unless it's a very minion heavy game. Don't play get the scoop, it's pretty usless. And keep easy encounter cards under her so you can use eidatic memory.

video_title: Silk Review for Marvel Champions The Card Game
text:
Silk is what Peter Parker Spiderman was always supposed to be.  She too is my favorite hero.  Soo Good! 

Some of the features you described as weaknesses are, IMHO, simply part of her style.  Often times in the first deck pass I simply cannot get all the upgrades on the board due to prioritizing maintaining a winnable board state.

That's what makes her decisions so meaningful.

Now that you pointed it out, I also really appreciate that she has a genuine "weakness" more like a lack of dominance against villains with multiple mod sets.

video_title: Silk Review for Marvel Champions The Card Game
text: Silk makes me mad. I never see him to get a treachery when I want one. Can't tuck in AE, and I never have the right upgrade out when I needed it.

video_title: Silk Review for Marvel Champions The Card Game
text: I am always playing double hand and i tried to pair her up with the Storm. I found out that she takes quite a while to setup and when paired with Storm who is really strong from beggining she feels really slugish and sometimes game ends before she even is able to setup. I must play her a bit more with other heroes but i am afraid now that while she may be strong by the end it may take a bit too long when played with other heroes. Or maybe Storm is way to strong.

video_title: The Problem With Mushroom Vapes #goblin #gobcast #zaza
text: The mushroom products at smoke shops are typically amanita muscaria labeled as "mushroom nootropics" which acts as a Gaba agonist like Xanax but trippier tho weaker and has some dissociative effects similar to ketamine and a bit of deliriant effects like Benadryl or nutmeg would give. If you have tried mixing a low dose of DXM and Benadryl the effects are a bit more similar to that than it is to shrooms. If you are going to try it don't vape it as studies haven't been done on the effects the things in the vape do to your lungs but if you eat edibles of it or the boiled mushroom "not raw" it's safer than vaping it.

video_title: VEGAN Steak (Honest Review)
text: Veggies truly shine on their own. I dont follow any system of belief when eating, just whatever tastes good and makes me feel good, so I eat about 85% veggies. Every time I’ve tried a vegan meat substitute, I wish I’d just gotten meat instead because it simply is part of a balanced diet, and they don’t taste the same at all.Something as simple as kale, throw it in a pan with some water to steam it. Once the waters gone, turn up the heat and add butter, garlic, and your favorite seasonings with a splash of savory sauce like Worcestershire or Soy sauce. Takes like 10 minutes to make a huge pan full of delicious greens, tender yet still with a bite to it, and packed with almost everything the body needs.

video_title: Rosemary water mistakes #shorts
text:
1.Not Boiling Enough:
If you do not boil rosemary water properly, its goodness will not be fully extracted. So make sure to boil it for 20 minutes.
2. Applying on oily scalp:
If you've oiled your hair, your scalp will be covered in oil, so rosemary water won't be able to penetrate effectively.
3. Keeping it for too long:
If you keep rosemary water in the fridge, you can store it for a maximum of 7 days, and without refrigeration, you can store it for a maximum of 3days.
Keeping it for longer increases the chances of rosemary water going bad.
4.Using it despite changes in its smell and appearance:
If your rosemary water's smell or appearance changes, it's a sign that it has spoiled. Stop using it.
5.Sleeping Right After Applying:
After applying rosemary water, avoid sleeping with wet hair. Because sleeping with wet hair increases the chances of dandruff as a damp scalp creates an ideal environment for dandruff-inducing bacteria. So, wait until your scalp is dry before going to bed
Bonus Points
6.Using Hot Rosemary Water:
After making rosemary water, make sure it comes to normal temperature before using it. Applying hot rosemary water can strip off the natural oils from your hair, leading to dryness.

7.Adding rosemary essential oil to rosemary water:
Many people add rosemary essential oil to rosemary water. However, water and oil do noting mix together, and when you mix them,rosemary essential oil forms a layer on top. When you spray it, this highly concentrated rosemary essential oil goes directly onto your scalp, which is not good at all

video_title: The benefits of milling your own flour🍞 #grain #milling #flour #homestead #organic #nutrition
text:
I have a great recipe for bread made from freshly milled flour. The key is to let the flour absorb the water for about 2 hours BEFORE adding yeast. The bran is more dense and needs more time to absorb water, which can make your dough feel like it needs more flour, but that often leads to very dry bread. It actually just needs time for the bran to hydrate and then the dough will be workable. Here’s my recipe (four loaves at a time which I then just freeze, so you can divide it if you try.)

For 4 Loaves:
    * 4 cups water
    * 2 cups milk - heated until steaming
    * 1/2 cup oil
    * 1/2 cup honey or brown sugar
    * 2 eggs
    * 4 tablespoons salt
    * 70 oz wheat - freshly milled
    * 3 tablespoons yeast (later)

Directions 
- add everything to your stand mixer except the yeast and mix on low for about 2 minutes until just combined. 
- rest for 2 hours, then add the yeast and mix on medium speed for about 10 minutes. Turn it out onto an oiled surface and knead any dry bits in, then rest about 10 minutes (not any longer because you want to save the yeast’s energy for the big rise.
- shape your loaves and add them to an buttered bread pan. Let rise for about 45 minutes (varies based on temps. 
- bake at 375 for 40 minutes for a loaf, 20 minutes for a pan of rolls. 

Let me know if you try it or have questions. It took me a long time to figure out this recipe but now no one can tell I’ve made it with freshly milled flour and it’s a big hit!

video_title: The benefits of milling your own flour🍞 #grain #milling #flour #homestead #organic #nutrition
text:
Hello from Bangalore, India.

It is very normal for us to get our flours milled. There are small scale flour mills in every locality, run by local families. We can take our grains and get them milled as per our preference, for a small fee. This is a traditional practice for as long as I can remember, and that is over six decades. 

Packaged flours, stripped of outer bran and most nutrients are also easily available everywhere. But the traditional method continues to be very popular.

Now we have domestic flour grinders similar to what you show here, though different in design and capacity etc, and slowly they are gaining popularity. These would help those who don’t have time to go to local flour mills, or those who really would like to make very small quantities of flour at home. 

Definitely breads - eastern flat breads or western baked breads - taste better and are healthier when made from whole grain flours. 

Thank you for posting this video.

video_title: #ad My Coconut Chia Pudding Meal Prep 🥥🫐 @Harmlessharvest   #harmlessharvest #mealprep
text:
Darlings, you’re gonna want to try this recipe!! It’s super easy and the Harmless Harvest coconut yogurt makes it so creamy and delicious. Harmless Harvest is now available at Walmart!

Blueberry Compote 
- 2 cups fresh blueberries 
- ¼ cup maple syrup 
- 2 tablespoons water 
- 1 tablespoon fresh lemon juice

Boil 1 cup blueberries with remaining ingredients. Add final cup of blueberries and simmer for 8 mins. Let cool. 

Chia Pudding : 
- 1/2 cup harmless harvest Coconut pudding 
- 1/2 tspn vanilla extract 
- 1-1/2 TBSP chia seeds 
Mix ingredients together then top with blueberry compote.
